documents_processed: 80201-00.pdf (technical drawing, 3 sheets, used only to verify Pos.Nr-to-balloon correspondence)
findings_for_cross_analysis: Stadler Pos.Nr and the Inventor xlsx Item hierarchy are unrelated numbering systems: cross-referencing 88 shared Sachnummern found only 2 coincidental matches (both at the top of the tree), 86 mismatches. Confirmed against the actual 80201-00 drawing PDF: balloon callouts on the drawing cover Pos.Nr 1-47 and 81-93 with zero gaps and zero numbers outside those ranges, confirming drawing-balloon == Stückliste Pos.Nr (the intended ISO drawing convention), while Inventor Item is a separate, CAD-export-order-driven numbering with no relationship to either.

Pos.Nr is scoped to (parent_assembly, position), not to the part itself: the same Sachnummer (e.g. 79101-21) has a different Pos.Nr depending which Stückliste it's listed in (Pos.7 in 80101-00, Pos.3 in 80201-00). Pos.Nr must be modeled as an edge/BOM-line attribute, never as a field on the global part record. Sachnummer remains the only identifier stable across drawing, Stückliste, and Inventor export.

Welds are first-class BOM line items with linear-meter quantities, not status flags on parts: 24 weld-type lines found across 3 welded assemblies (80101-00: 10, 80201-00: 13, 79102-00: 1), all referencing EN 15085-3 / ISO 14343 / filler 19-12-3-L-Si. This is a third distinct 'operations' modeling convention found in the BOM pipeline so far (alongside GST's per-part status columns), supporting the org_operations per-org customization model over a fixed pipeline.

Insulation sub-assemblies (80110-00, 80210-00) use a fourth part-numbering convention: the 'Sachnummer' field is a sequential local label ('Szigetelés 01'/'Szigetelés 1') with no relationship to Stadler's drawing-number system, distinguished only by an Armaflex thickness-coded SKU (RA-19-99, RA-09-99, RA-06-99) plus a tape SKU (RA-TAPE). These local labels are not globally unique or stable identifiers.

Bought/standard hardware (ISO/DIN-defined bolts, nuts, washers, sealants, pastes) appears only at the 'vollst.' (complete) wrapper level, never inside a 'Geschweisst' welded-shell sub-BOM -- a clean separation between fabricated content and bought/consumable content in this source's convention.

Two source-data anomalies found, not corrected: (1) 80101-00 Pos.18 (part 80101-11) shows Werkstoff norm 'EN 10051' where every other 1.4301/1.4404 row in the same document shows 'EN 10088-2' -- likely a copy-paste error in Stadler's own file. (2) 79102-00 is the only one of 8 BOM documents written in English instead of German/Hungarian, despite its own title block still reading 'de/hu' (and contains Stadler's own typo, 'Maintaince').

Mass precision: 'vollst.' wrapper BOMs reference child assembly masses at higher decimal precision (e.g. 87.03, 162.12) than those same assemblies' own header totals when rounded for display (87, 162). Not a conflict -- recommend using the higher-precision child-reference value over the rounded header total when both exist.

Masse [kg] is consistently a per-unit value, not a line total, across all 8 Stadler documents -- confirmed by summing qty x unit-mass against the page-1 header total for 80201-00 (162.04 vs stated 162) and 80101-00 (matches 87 within rounding).

The xlsx export's Description column is empty for all 168 rows; German/Hungarian Benennung only exists in the 8 Stückliste PDFs and the Zeichnungsliste, and even those only cover drawing-numbered parts -- the 66 standard-hardware/insulation/consumable lines have no description source beyond the Stückliste Benennung text captured in this fixture.
